description: FIND-066 was only partially resolved. The architectural fix (RD-021 DTO blocks field, reducer mirroring) is in place, but EditorPanel.handleSnapshot still contains a fallback path that fabricates a synthetic block from snapshot.focusedBlockId when (a) the snapshot has no `blocks` field AND (b) the focused block does not exist in viewState.blocks. The inline comment self-identifies this as test-mode scaffolding: 'This path fires only for test snapshots that predate RD-021 (no blocks in DTO).' This is the same anti-pattern as FIND-060 (production code carrying test-harness scaffolding) — production code retains a path that exists solely so legacy tests pass. RD-021 explicitly states the reducer 'preserves currentBlocks when absent' — it does NOT authorise the shell to invent blocks from focusedBlockId. With every test now passing initialBlocks plus snapshots whose focusedBlockId matches a seeded block, this path can be deleted with no test loss.
suggestedRemediation: Delete the legacy fallback block (lines 129-147). Verify by running `bun run test:dom` — if any test fails, update that test to either inject `initialBlocks` covering its focusedBlockId or to emit a snapshot whose `blocks` field carries the expected entry (RD-021 path). The shell must never synthesise block data; that authority belongs to the Rust domain via the DTO blocks projection.
